name: Blue Lagoon
description: Medicinal spa created from the mineral-rich runoff of a geothermal power plant

name: Svartifoss
description: The unique waterfall in Iceland's south features a backdrop of geometric black lava columns.

name: The Museum of Icelandic Sorcery & Witchcraft
description: Staves, storm-calling, and of course, necropants.

name: Hvítserkur
description: Rising from the sea like a stone monster.

name: Gljúfrabúi
description: This fantastical waterfall is hidden in a cave located in a cracked cliff.

name: Hallgrimskirkja
description: One of the tallest buildings in Iceland, and perhaps, the most visually impressive.

name: Kerið Crater Lake
description: This eye-popping Icelandic crater lake is surrounded by red volcanic rock.

name: Jökulsárlón
description: Iceland's largest lagoon is home to stunning multi-colored icebergs.

name: Fjaðrárgljúfur
description: Strange rock formations tower above the river that snakes through this enchanting Icelandic canyon.

name: Bridge Between Continents
description: Walk in the footsteps of the gods and straddle two tectonic plates.

name: Icelandic Phallological Museum
description: An encyclopedic collection of mammal penises.

name: The Arctic Henge
description: This modern monument to pagan belief looks like it was transported straight from ancient times.

name: Thrihnukagigur Volcano
description: Climb right into the heart of this colorful Iceland volcano.

name: Dettifoss
description: The most powerful waterfall in all of Europe.

name: Leiðarendi Lava Tube
description: A winding, blood-red tunnel of hardened lava navigable by foot.

name: Reykjadalur
description: Iceland's "Smoky Valley" is a beautiful river that is shrouded in a constant cover of steam clouds.

name: Godafoss
description: This elegant arc of a waterfall marks a religious turning point.

name: Álfaskólinn (Elf School)
description: Learn about Iceland's hidden folk at this school dedicated to the study of elves.

name: World's Largest Puffin Colony
description: Every summer local children form "pysja patrols" to save young birds that have wandered from their nests.

name: Skrímslasetrið
description: The Icelandic Sea Monster Museum explores the area's history of aquatic cryptid sightings.

name: Haukadalur Geothermal Field
description: This valley of hot springs and boiling mud pots is home to the record-holding "Geysir," which originated the English word.

name: Aldeyjarfoss
description: A little-visited waterfall in an otherworldly landscape.

name: Gullfoss (Golden Falls)
description: If a glacial river suddenly diving into a sheer chasm via a dramatic, multi-stage waterfall isn't peak Iceland, it's certainly close.

name: Icelandic Punk Museum
description: This tiny museum's sizable collection is jammed into a former underground public toilet.

name: Gjáin
description: Surrounded by ash fields in Iceland, an impossibly verdant valley looks like something out of a fairytale.

name: Whales of Iceland
description: The largest whale museum in Europe allows visitors to walk among life-size giants.

name: Seljavallalaug
description: The oldest (maybe) swimming pool in Iceland is a stunning oasis built into the side of a lush hill.

name: Hofskirkja
description: This fairytale-like structure is the last turf church ever constructed in Iceland.

name: Into The Glacier
description: Travel deep inside of Europe's second largest glacier in the world's largest man-made ice cave.

name: Grafarkirkja
description: The oldest church in Iceland is one of the few to feature ornate decoration.

name: Dimmuborgir
description: These ominous lava formations were said to be an entrance to the netherworld so of course they inspired a famous metal band.

name: U.S. Navy DC-3 Wreckage
description: This wrecked airplane has been sitting on a black sand beach since the 1970s.

name: Viking Village Film Set
description: This replica Viking village was created for a film that was never shot and is now open to visitors.

name: Silfra
description: This natural Icelandic fissure allows divers to swim right between two volatile tectonic plates.

name: Drangsnes Hot Tubs
description: A trio of hidden hot tubs takes advantage of Iceland's natural geothermal vents.

name: Snorralaug
description: This hobbit-y little hot spring may be the oldest spring used by humans in all of Iceland.

name: Reynisdrangar
description: Three columns of volcanic rock shoot out of the ocean.

name: 'How's It Going?' Phone Booth
description: An isolated, rusty phone booth commemorates Iceland's first international telegraph cable.

name: Hekla
description: A stratovolcano located in the south of Iceland, with a long history of charted activity and a rich mythology attached.

name: Látrabjarg
description: Puffin enthusiasts flock to Europe's largest seabird cliff, which over one million birds call home.

name: Hengifoss
description: The bright red stripes streaking across the rocks are the star feature at this Icelandic waterfall.

name: Þrídrangaviti Lighthouse
description: A lonely beacon, precariously perched atop a remote sliver of rock.

name: Krafla
description: This tumultuous Icelandic lava field looks so alien European researchers want to use it as a stand-in for Mars.

name: Búðakirkja
description: This black church sits alone among a field of lava rock.

name: Hallbjarnarstaðakambur
description: Three million year old fossils are exposed for all to see on this Icelandic shore.

name: Rauðfeldsgjá
description: This mountain fissure hides a secret path to a secluded mossy grotto within the rock itself.

name: Museum of Prophecies
description: This museum dedicated to divination also honors a local 10th-century prophetess.

name: Námaskarð
description: A sulfuric martian landscape that looks far better than it smells.

name: The Raven's Nest
description: This oceanside Reykjavik spot is speckled with recycled sculptures and large stones.

name: Snæfellsjökull
description: A formerly glacier-tipped volcano that inspired none other than Jules Verne.

name: Gunnuhver
description: Iceland's largest geothermal mud pool is a little bit haunted and a little bit dangerous.

name: Arctic Circle Marker
description: A giant concrete orb in far north Iceland marks the ever-shifting boundary.

name: Krýsuvíkurbjarg (Krýsuvík Cliffs)
description: The summer tourists on these cliffs are thousands of seabirds.

name: Selatangar
description: Eerie ruins of an abandoned medieval fishing village crumble atop an unforgiving stretch of earth.

name: Garðar BA 64
description: The oldest steel ship in Iceland has been beached for years and is now a rusting ruin.

name: Öskjuvatn and Viti Lakes
description: Two volcanic lakes in an enormous crater formed in a prehistoric eruption.

name: Draugasetrid
description: Inside this museum, visitors venture through ghost stories entrenched in Icelandic folklore.

name: Ásatrú Temple
description: Iceland's first pagan temple in 1,000 years is underway in Reykjavík.

name: Tvísöngur
description: These concrete domes were installed on an Icelandic bluff to honor a very specific musical tradion.

name: Harpa
description: This stunning concert hall has become a symbol of Iceland's national recovery.

name: The Library of Water
description: This collection of unique liquids allows visitors to wander the waters of Iceland's glaciers.

name: Red Chair
description: A giant red chair bolted to a rock on a remote mossy hillside in Iceland.

name: Hólavallagarður Cemetery
description: This verdant, tree-lined Reykjavik graveyard is both enchanting and eerie.

name: Fischersund
description: This aromatherapy shop and mini-museum cofounded by the Sigur Rós frontman documents Icelandic history through scents.

name: Grímsey Island
description: The Arctic Circle runs through this isolated spot north of Iceland.

name: Stuðlagil Canyon
description: This canyon is comprised of gorgeous waters and magnificent basalt columns.

name: Keldur Hall
description: The oldest hall in Iceland was written of in the Sagas and features a centuries-old escape tunnel.

name: The Eggs of Merry Bay
description: These enlarged egg sculptures represent the native birds of eastern Iceland.

name: Friðheimar
description: Inside this Icelandic greenhouse, geothermal energy keeps tomatoes warm and growing.

name: Berserkjahraun
description: This scenic Icelandic lava flow is named for a pair of Berserkers who were murdered, according to a famous Saga.

name: Randulff’s Sea House
description: This remote eatery and museum is preserved as a time capsule of a small Icelandic fishing town.

name: Drangurinn Rock
description: Icelandic folklore says this giant boulder was ripped from the mountains and placed at its current location, where it became home to elves.

name: Námafjall
description: In Northeastern Iceland lies something of an oxymoron: a steaming desert.

name: Abandoned Djúpavík Herring Factory
description: This rusting remnant of the local herring industry is being given a second life.

name: Ancient Kjolur Trail
description: Outlaws, hot springs, and tragedy have all left their mark on this ancient road in the Icelandic highlands.

name: Akureyri Heart-Shaped Traffic Lights
description: Instead of solid circles, the stoplights in this northern Icelandic town have glowing red hearts.

name: Monument to the Unknown Bureaucrat
description: Iceland's tribute to its thankless civil servants.

name: Laufskálavarða
description: Cairns built to bring travelers good luck stretch across the land like miniature stone mountains.

name: Christmas House
description: Iceland: home to volcanoes, waterfalls and, apparently, Santa?

name: Flateyri Bookstore
description: The oldest bookstore in Iceland.

name: Eiriksstadir
description: A fantastic reconstruction of a 10th-century Viking longhouse.

name: Sigurgeirs Bird Museum
description: Largest private bird collection in Iceland, formed from a tragic accident.

name: Hvannadalshnjúkur
description: Climbing Iceland's highest peak on the rim of the Oræfajökull volcano.

name: Drangey Island
description: An Icelandic island full of diving birds and legend.

name: Piece of Berlin Wall In Iceland
description: A bit of the Berlin Wall was gifted to the Icelandic capital to commemorate the 25th anniversary of German reunification.

name: Halldórsskora (Elephant Rock)
description: On an island south of Iceland, a large volcanic rock formation resembles an elephant.

name: Sören and Sigurborgar's Rare Stone Collection
description: This elderly couple's hand-picked collection of rare gems is one of the finest in all of Iceland.

name: Þingvellir
description: The picturesque birthplace of Icelandic democracy is drenched in the blood of its brutal past.

name: Samúel Jónsson's Art Farm
description: This secluded Iceland farm holds the collected works of one rural sculptor's creations.

name: Bæjarins Beztu Pylsur
description: One of the oldest family businesses in Reykjavík serves lamb-based hot dogs.

name: Svörtuloft Lighthouse
description: A Wes Anderson-esque lighthouse that boasts magnificent coastal views.

name: Þingvellir Church
description: This beautiful, historic church looks like something out of a simpler past.

name: South Iceland's Bra Fence
description: Along this wire fence, travelers have been leaving a particular memento since 2012.

name: Elliðaey Island Lodge
description: Internet rumors, often involving Icelandic singer Björk, have abounded regarding this isolated outcrop of rock.

name: Bjarnarhöfn Shark Museum
description: At this museum in Iceland, visitors can sample fermented Greenland shark.

name: Volcanic Island of Heimaey
description: A modern Nordic Pompeii on the Icelandic island of Heimaey.

name: Selárdalur Valley
description: A "simple-hearted artist" turned an isolated Icelandic valley into his own art museum.

name: Giantess Cave
description: A popular Icelandic children's story comes to life inside this cave.

name: The Stone Carvings of Páll Guðmundsson
description: A modern artist's primitive stone faces and unique rock instrument join Iceland's terrain with its history.

name: Giant Viking Beer Can
description: Akureyri is home to an oversized can of one of Iceland's favourite beers.

name: Diamond Beach
description: A black sand beach where glacial fragments wash ashore and look like sparkling diamonds.

name: Skemmtigarðurinn Grafarvogi
description: This Viking-inspired miniature golf course features a pirate ship and views of Icelandic landscapes.

name: Rakkaberg
description: This natural rock formation features seemingly unnatural vertical ridges that have been blamed on elves.

name: 'Víddaflakk' ('Interdimensional Hopscotch')
description: This plaque tells the story of an event that took place in an imagined parallel universe.

name: Skeiðará Bridge Monument
description: Twisted girders are all that remain of an Icelandic bridge that was wiped out by a combination of volcanic heat and glacial floods.

name: The Freezer
description: A world-class theater sits hidden in this remote town in West Iceland.

name: Petra's Stone and Mineral Collection
description: One woman's lifelong collection is on display in this unassuming house on an Icelandic fjord.

name: Geysir Glíma Restaurant
description: A restaurant devoted to "trouser grip" wrestling, an ancient form of Viking wrestling that is Iceland's national sport.

name: Hveragerði Sunnumӧrk Shopping Center
description: The Hveragerði fissure runs through this unassuming shopping center.

name: Deildartunguhver
description: Europe’s most powerful hot spring supplies the surrounding region with hot water.

name: Glaumbær Farm & Museum
description: This former farm In northern Iceland is now a museum, where visitors can tour beautifully-preserved turf houses.

name: Hversdagssafn
description: Discover the magic in the mundane in Iceland's Museum of Everyday Life.

name: EVE Online Monument
description: This monument to one of the largest science fiction games of all time bears the names of hundreds of thousands of players.

name: Öndvegissúlur
description: A replica of the decorated wooden pillars Viking sailors threw overboard when finding new places to establish a settlement.

name: Hveradalir Geothermal Area
description: A geothermal paradise with bubbling mud pools and bright blue hot springs.

name: Grave of Bobby Fischer
description: The final resting place of the controversial chess champion.

name: Uppspretta
description: This wild explosion of color on a disused water tower tells the whimsical tale of a curious puffin.

name: Kirkjugólf Basalt Columns
description: What was once believed to be a church floor turned out to be the artwork of geology.

name: Reykjavík 871±2
description: The exhibition explores the Icelandic city's Viking Age history.

name: Murals of Hellissandur
description: Dozens of large, brightly-colored paintings adorn the walls of the "street art capital of Iceland."

name: Húsafell Lifting Stone
description: Pick up this rock and become an instant legend.

name: Kjarvalshvammur
description: This tiny cabin was home to one of Iceland's most famous painters and was the only land he ever owned.

name: Grettislaug
description: A geothermal pool in North Iceland that is said to be the bathing spot of the legendary Grettir the Strong.

name: Heinabergslón
description: A little-known, yet charming glacial lagoon.

name: 'The Black Cone, Monument to Civil Disobedience'
description: A monument dedicated to the civil uprisings that took place during the Pots and Pans Revolution.

name: Flatey Island
description: Once the site of a 12-century monastery, this tiny island is now home to only five permanent residents.

name: Draugarétt (Ghost Fold)
description: Though they may seem like simple stones, these rocks represent a ghostly legend.

name: Great Auk Sculpture at Valahnúkamöl
description: This five-foot-tall bronze bird is a memorial to a species that was hunted out of existence.

name: Herring Era Museum
description: This excellent museum recreates the boom days of the herring rush in the "Atlantic Klondike."

name: ‘Silver Sabler’
description: A relatively obscure antihero from the Marvel Comics universe adorns a wall inside Iceland's main airport.

name: Jaja Ding Dong
description: A real-life homage to the Rachel McAdams and Will Ferrell's fictional Eurovision musical duo.

name: Sólheimar Ecovillage
description: This educational and eco-friendly community is Iceland's "home of the sun."

name: Þúfa
description: This earthwork installation sits on a grassy hill, celebrating Iceland's past and present.

name: Beached Whalers
description: Two whaling vessels that were sunk by an anti-whaling organization in 1986 now sit on the shore of this beautiful fjord.

name: Eldheimar Museum
description: This museum explores how a 1973 volcanic eruption forever changed this small Icelandic island.

name: 'Heavier Mountain'
description: The only structure in a remote mountain pass, these colorful cubes are visible from miles away.

name: Árbær Open Air Museum
description: The history of Reykjavik told through a historical village and live actors.

name: Arnessyla Heritage Museum
description: This collection of several museums includes one of the oldest structures in Iceland.

name: Einar Jónsson Museum
description: An unusual building on a "desolate hill" features the works of an Icelandic sculptor.

name: Ingibjörg H. Bjarnason Statue
description: The first woman to become a member of the parliament of Iceland.

name: Glymur
description: A rugged hike leads to a breathtaking waterfall, once thought to be the tallest in Iceland.

name: Karl og Kerling
description: Legend has it these two stones are petrified trolls.

name: Katla Ice Caves
description: These unique and ever-changing caves are a wonder of Iceland.

name: Vík í Myrdal Church
description: A tiny village church in dramatic surroundings.

name: Sun Voyager
description: This boat represents the promise of undiscovered territory, a dream of hope, and progress and freedom for Iceland.

name: The Yoda Cave
description: This cave resembles the Star Wars Jedi master.

name: Steðji / Staupasteinn
description: A goblet-shaped rock that, according to lore, is the home of an elven hermit.

name: Holdsveikraspítalinn í Laugarnesi
description: The remains of a hospital that housed over 200 leprosy patients.

name: Skógar Museum
description: A museum celebrating Icelandic history and technology.

name: Keflavik F4-Phantom
description: A tribute to the successful role of the Keflavik Naval Air Station in the defense of Iceland, United States and NATO Alliance.

name: Eyrarland Statue
description: Does this statue depict Thor, or Jesus Christ? Nobody knows.

name: Akureyri Domino’s Pizza
description: Enjoy a slice under the midnight sun at the world’s northermost Domino’s.

name: Aircraft Mechanics Creed Monument
description: An airplane wing is a roadside art installation and monument on a remote stretch of highway.

name: Óshlíð
description: One of the world’s most dangerous roads may be this abandoned shore route in Iceland.

name: Apollo Astronaut Training Memorial
description: This monument commemorates NASA’s astronaut training in northern Iceland.

name: Grjótagjá
description: This volcanic cave lake is essentially hard proof that Iceland is the most magical place on Earth.

name: Búri
description: One of the most remarkable caves in all of Iceland was created by a once raging river of lava.

name: Freddi Arcade & Toys Museum
description: A vintage video game wonderland in downtown Reykjavik.

name: Dularfulla Búðin
description: Iceland's only steampunk shop imagines a retro-futuristic world that never was.

name: The Volcano Show at Red Rock Cinema
description: A charmingly eccentric magma chaser presents his complete history of the island's eruptions since 1947, in cinematic form, just for you.

name: Hveragerdi Bus Stop
description: This abandoned, partly demolished building is now home to beautiful graffiti and murals.
